Finding: CutSpec.full is the maximal carrier route
Static observation: Finite/CutSpec.lean defines full T with carrier := T.carrier and full_carrier is rfl.
Risk: Confusing CutSpec.full with IdealThread or with a diagonal/scalar route would erase the intended sector/interior/interface geometry.
Refactoring consequence: Add S08 and require Phase 3.0 boundary-route separation before neutral Schur/DtN wrappers.

Static observation: A_to_B exports carrier, Schur, regionNet, split, DtN, selectedLevel, and regime data. Inputs B/C/D/E_to_A exist as empty/small structures.
Risk: Pillar A could accidentally treat its own outputs as sources.
Refactoring consequence: The access matrix enforces: A consumes only inputs; outputs are forbidden-to-A.

Finding: Blue objects are proof-carrying but not public-final
Static observation: Object status B rows occur in Cayley-Dickson, Schur/DtN, and operational arithmetic families. These are already derived-only/proof-carrying in current code, but still semantically/API unfinished.
Risk: Treating blue objects as final because proofs exist would export old SM/AR semantics or wrong API boundaries into Phase 3+ and handoffs.
Refactoring consequence: Add Phase 1.1.1.2 and LEG7. Every blue object must get a correction ledger entry: semantic defect, natural correction phase, allowed temporary consumers, forbidden public consumers, and exit-to-white condition.

Static observation: ToC/Contract.lean defines IdealThread.carrier L as {U.cell L}; IdealThread.family uses that carrier; deterministic coarsen_carrier_eq_prev proves coarsenSet of the next carrier is the previous singleton.
Risk: Any plan that uses an existing IdealThread route as a nontrivial boundary matrix source would silently collapse to scalar/degenerate data.
Refactoring consequence: Add S07 and BR06. Later full-boundary/DtN phases must explicitly reject IdealThread as full matrix source unless a separate lifting theorem is supplied.
